result.summary: Alba Cristina Magalhaes Alves de Melo obtained her PhD degree in Computer Science from the Institut National Polytechnique de Grenoble (INPG), France, in 1996. In 2008, she was a PostDoc Researcher at the University of Ottawa, Canada; in 2011 she was invited as a Guest Scientist at the Université Paris-Sud, France; in 2013 she did a sabbatical at the Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya, Spain and in 2018 she was invited as a Guest Scientist at the Institut National Polytechnique de Grenoble, France. Since 1997, she is with the Department of Computer Science of the University of Brasilia (UnB) where she is now Full Professor and Head of the Department. Prof. Melo is Member of the Council of the Brazilian Computer Society (2021-2025), Vice-chair of the IEEE Technical Committee in Parallel Processing and Member of the Brazilian Delegation for the BRICS Working Group on High Performance Computing and Information and Communication Technologies (BRICS WG on HPC-ICT). Her research interests include High Performance Computing, Accelerators (GPU, FPGA), Bioinformatics and Clouds. One of the PhD Thesis advised by Prof. Melo was awarded the Best PhD Thesis in Computer Science in Brazil (Premio Capes de Tese) in 2016. She is also recipient of the 2023 IEEE TCPP Outstanding Service and Contributions Award and the 2019 Wilkes Award for the Best Paper published at The Computer Journal in 2018. Prof. Melo is a Senior Member of the IEEE Society and the IEEE Computer Society. She was Associate Editor of IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems (2015-2019) and she is currently Associate Editor of IEEE Transactions on Computers, Journal of Parallel and Distributed Computing, Future Generations Computing Systems and ACM Computing Surveys. Prof. Melo was co-General Chair of IEEE IPDPS 2024. She served as Program Chair at HiPEAC 2025, co-Program Chair at IEEE/ACM CCGrid 2025, co-Program Chair in IEEE IPDPS 2019 and IEEE SBAC-PAD 2017. In addition, she served as co-Track Chair in several prestigious conferences such as Euro-Par 2025, IEEE Cluster 2023, Supercomputing (SC) 2022, IEEE IPDPS 2022, Euro-Par 2020.Prof. Melo was keynote speaker at prestigious conferences (Euro-Par 2021, SBAC-PAD 2023 and CARLA 2020) and in several international workshops. She coordinated international collaboration projects with Spain and Canada and participates/participated in several international projects with the European Commission, France and Germany, among others. (Texto informado pelo autor)

items.description: The main goal of the project is to investigate fault tolerance techniques for biological sequence comparison applications. In a previous joint cooperation between the BSC team and the University of Brasilia team, the version of the tool CUDAlign 4.0 was designed. However, fault tolerance was only implemented in its single GPU version. Introducing fault tolerance in the multi-GPU version has (a) the challenge of achieving a global consistent state that has a reasonable overhead; (b) designing a rollback strategy that reverts to a considerably recent consistent state.. items.description: The main objective of this project is to provide researchers with a framework for the efficient execution of biotechnology applications on multiple Amazon AWS cloud platforms. The framework should provide better solutions to the resource management problem for running high-performance applications in the AWS cloud. To this end, we will address the problems of dynamic scaling of virtual machines and elastic containers aiming at minimizing the use of resources, execution time, energy savings and maximizing availability without violating contracted service level agreements. We will also investigate efficient parallel and distributed strategies adapted to the execution of target applications in the cloud.. Situação: Concluído; Natureza: Pesquisa. items.description: O presente Projeto de Cooperação Internacional integrará os esforços da UPC e da UnB de maneira a atingir os seguintes objetivos: (a) Investigação de melhorias nos modelos de programação propostos pelos pesquisadores da UPC, que sejam adaptados aos problemas de Bioinformática e, em particular, aos problemas de genômica comparativa. As melhorias propostas gerarão novas construções paralelas, que serão integradas a diferentes plataformas de hardware (GPU, FPGA ou CellBE), em função da experiência prévia da UPC e da UnB; (b) Concepção de uma implementação MASA-GPU-OmpSs, MASA-GPU-OpenCL e de uma implementação MASA-IntelPhi-OpenCL; (d) Criação de uma nova versão do MASA, que permita que uma mesma comparação utilize múltiplos elementos de computação como por exemplo, um cluster de GPUs, onde as CPUs e GPUs que integram o cluster possam ser utilizados em conjunto; (d) Definição de outras aplicações de bioinformática que possam se executar segundo a arquitetura MASA; (e) Estudo teórico da principal otimização proposta no CUDAlign 2.1 (block pruning), nas suas duas variantes; (f) Publicações conjuntas em periódicos e conferencias nas áreas de Bioinformática e Ciência da Computação.. Situação: Concluído; Natureza: Pesquisa. Alunos envolvidos: Graduação: (3) / Mestrado acadêmico: (3) / Doutorado: (1) . items.description: This International Cooperation Project involves three research areas: High Performance Computing, Data Storage/Retrieval and Bioinformatics applications. It will integrate the efforts of the INRIA-Saclay, Mines ParisTech, Universidade de Brasília, Universidade Federal Fluminense, Embrapa-Cenargen and Universidad de Santiago de Chile in order to achieve the following goals: (a) Investigate which characteristics of Bioinformatics Applications have to be considered when choosing/proposing a Federated Cloud infrastructure. Characteristics such as large-scale data management, workflow orchestration/integration and resource federation will be studied in detail; (b) Investigate and propose new Bioinformatics tools that are adapted to the Federated Cloud environment; (c) Investigate and propose new Bioinformatics algorithms that deal with huge data sets, aiming to reduce execution time and/or the amount of memory needed, making them suitable for Federated Cloud Computing environments; (d) Investigate which performance metrics must be taken into consideration when running applications on a Federated Cloud environment; (e) Joint publications in Journals and Conferences in the areas of Computer Science and Bioinformatics.. Situação: Concluído; Natureza: Pesquisa. Alunos envolvidos: Graduação: (3) / Mestrado acadêmico: (2) / Doutorado: (1) . content.text:
The candidate’s background is strongly aligned with the project **“Execução Paralela de Aplicações de Bioinformática em Plataformas de Alto Desempenho”**.

Why it matches:
- **Academic formation:** PhD in Computer Science, with dissertation on **parallel machines / memory consistency models**, plus a master’s in computing focused on **parallel process management**.
- **Expertise:** explicitly includes **Parallel Programming**, **High Performance Computing**, **system architecture**, and **bioinformatics / bioengineering**.
- **Research lines:** one line is exactly about **“Computação em Ambientes Heterogêneos para Aplicações de Bioinformática”**, and another about **shared-memory and message-passing programming in computer clusters**.
- **Professional experience:** has worked at **UnB since 1997**, with long-term research activity in the Department of Computer Science, including a line on **bioinformatics applications in heterogeneous environments**.
- **Prior projects:** has already coordinated or participated in multiple projects directly related to the theme, including:
  - **“Execução Paralela Integrada de Aplicações de Bioinformática em Plataformas Heterogêneas de Alto Desempenho”**
  - **“Programação Paralela Eficiente de Algoritmos de Bioinformática...”**
  - **“Comparação de Sequências Biológicas em Plataformas Híbridas”**
  - **“Comparação de sequências biológicas em clusters de computadores”**

In short, the candidate has a **longstanding track record in HPC, parallel/distributed systems, accelerators, and bioinformatics**, which is highly consistent with the project’s focus.

result.name: Alba Cristina Magalhães Alves de Melo
result.summary: Alba Cristina Magalhaes Alves de Melo obtained her PhD degree in Computer Science from the Institut National Polytechnique de Grenoble (INPG), France, in 1996. In 2008, she was a PostDoc Researcher at the University of Ottawa, Canada; in 2011 she was invited as a Guest Scientist at the Université Paris-Sud, France; in 2013 she did a sabbatical at the Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya, Spain and in 2018 she was invited as a Guest Scientist at the Institut National Polytechnique de Grenoble, France. Since 1997, she is with the Department of Computer Science of the University of Brasilia (UnB) where she is now Full Professor and Head of the Department. Prof. Melo is Member of the Council of the Brazilian Computer Society (2021-2025), Vice-chair of the IEEE Technical Committee in Parallel Processing and Member of the Brazilian Delegation for the BRICS Working Group on High Performance Computing and Information and Communication Technologies (BRICS WG on HPC-ICT). Her research interests include High Performance Computing, Accelerators (GPU, FPGA), Bioinformatics and Clouds. One of the PhD Thesis advised by Prof. Melo was awarded the Best PhD Thesis in Computer Science in Brazil (Premio Capes de Tese) in 2016. She is also recipient of the 2023 IEEE TCPP Outstanding Service and Contributions Award and the 2019 Wilkes Award for the Best Paper published at The Computer Journal in 2018. Prof. Melo is a Senior Member of the IEEE Society and the IEEE Computer Society. She was Associate Editor of IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems (2015-2019) and she is currently Associate Editor of IEEE Transactions on Computers, Journal of Parallel and Distributed Computing, Future Generations Computing Systems and ACM Computing Surveys. Prof. Melo was co-General Chair of IEEE IPDPS 2024. She served as Program Chair at HiPEAC 2025, co-Program Chair at IEEE/ACM CCGrid 2025, co-Program Chair in IEEE IPDPS 2019 and IEEE SBAC-PAD 2017. In addition, she served as co-Track Chair in several prestigious conferences such as Euro-Par 2025, IEEE Cluster 2023, Supercomputing (SC) 2022, IEEE IPDPS 2022, Euro-Par 2020.Prof. Melo was keynote speaker at prestigious conferences (Euro-Par 2021, SBAC-PAD 2023 and CARLA 2020) and in several international workshops. She coordinated international collaboration projects with Spain and Canada and participates/participated in several international projects with the European Commission, France and Germany, among others. (Texto informado pelo autor)

items.description: The main goal of the project is to investigate fault tolerance techniques for biological sequence comparison applications. In a previous joint cooperation between the BSC team and the University of Brasilia team, the version of the tool CUDAlign 4.0 was designed. However, fault tolerance was only implemented in its single GPU version. Introducing fault tolerance in the multi-GPU version has (a) the challenge of achieving a global consistent state that has a reasonable overhead; (b) designing a rollback strategy that reverts to a considerably recent consistent state.. items.description: O presente Projeto de Cooperação Internacional integrará os esforços da UPC e da UnB de maneira a atingir os seguintes objetivos: (a) Investigação de melhorias nos modelos de programação propostos pelos pesquisadores da UPC, que sejam adaptados aos problemas de Bioinformática e, em particular, aos problemas de genômica comparativa. As melhorias propostas gerarão novas construções paralelas, que serão integradas a diferentes plataformas de hardware (GPU, FPGA ou CellBE), em função da experiência prévia da UPC e da UnB; (b) Concepção de uma implementação MASA-GPU-OmpSs, MASA-GPU-OpenCL e de uma implementação MASA-IntelPhi-OpenCL; (d) Criação de uma nova versão do MASA, que permita que uma mesma comparação utilize múltiplos elementos de computação como por exemplo, um cluster de GPUs, onde as CPUs e GPUs que integram o cluster possam ser utilizados em conjunto; (d) Definição de outras aplicações de bioinformática que possam se executar segundo a arquitetura MASA; (e) Estudo teórico da principal otimização proposta no CUDAlign 2.1 (block pruning), nas suas duas variantes; (f) Publicações conjuntas em periódicos e conferencias nas áreas de Bioinformática e Ciência da Computação.. Situação: Concluído; Natureza: Pesquisa. Alunos envolvidos: Graduação: (3) / Mestrado acadêmico: (3) / Doutorado: (1) . Integrantes: Alba Cristina Magalhães Alves de Melo - Coordenador / Edans Flavius de Oliveira Sandes - Integrante / Eduard Ayguade - Integrante / Mateo Valero - Integrante / MARTORELL, XAVIER - Integrante / George Teodoro - Integrante / Marco Antonio Caldas de Figueiredo Jr - Integrante. Financiador(es): Coordenação de Aperfeiçoamento de Pessoal de Nível Superior - Auxílio financeiro.

items.description: This International Cooperation Project involves three research areas: High Performance Computing, Data Storage/Retrieval and Bioinformatics applications. It will integrate the efforts of the INRIA-Saclay, Mines ParisTech, Universidade de Brasília, Universidade Federal Fluminense, Embrapa-Cenargen and Universidad de Santiago de Chile in order to achieve the following goals: (a) Investigate which characteristics of Bioinformatics Applications have to be considered when choosing/proposing a Federated Cloud infrastructure. Characteristics such as large-scale data management, workflow orchestration/integration and resource federation will be studied in detail; (b) Investigate and propose new Bioinformatics tools that are adapted to the Federated Cloud environment; (c) Investigate and propose new Bioinformatics algorithms that deal with huge data sets, aiming to reduce execution time and/or the amount of memory needed, making them suitable for Federated Cloud Computing environments; (d) Investigate which performance metrics must be taken into consideration when running applications on a Federated Cloud environment; (e) Joint publications in Journals and Conferences in the areas of Computer Science and Bioinformatics.. Situação: Concluído; Natureza: Pesquisa. Alunos envolvidos: Graduação: (3) / Mestrado acadêmico: (2) / Doutorado: (1) . Integrantes: Alba Cristina Magalhães Alves de Melo - Integrante / Maria Emilia Telles Walter - Coordenador / Celia Ghedini Ralha - Integrante / Alessandro Ferreira Leite - Integrante / Lucia Drummond - Integrante / Claude Tadonki - Integrante / Christine Eisenbeis - Integrante / Mauricio Marin - Integrante / Roberto Togawa - Integrante. items.description: The main objective of this project is to provide researchers with a framework for the efficient execution of biotechnology applications on multiple Amazon AWS cloud platforms. The framework should provide better solutions to the resource management problem for running high-performance applications in the AWS cloud. To this end, we will address the problems of dynamic scaling of virtual machines and elastic containers aiming at minimizing the use of resources, execution time, energy savings and maximizing availability without violating contracted service level agreements. We will also investigate efficient parallel and distributed strategies adapted to the execution of target applications in the cloud.. Situação: Concluído; Natureza: Pesquisa. Alunos envolvidos: Graduação: (1) / Mestrado acadêmico: (3) / Doutorado: (7) . Financiador(es): Conselho Nacional de Desenvolvimento Científico e Tecnológico - Outra.
items.name: CNPq/AWS - BioCloud - A Framework for the Execution of Biotechnology Applications in Several Platforms of the Amazon AWS Cloud
